revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:二塚山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.602216666667|N|134.13393611111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Futatsukayama Kofun |image=
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |alternate_name=Futatsuka Kofun{{Sfn|Ushimado Town History Materials II|1997|pp=174-184}} / Sōzuka Kofun{{Sfn|Ushimado Town History Materials II|1997|pp=174-184}} |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|qid=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|en|Setouchi, Okayama|qid=Q691691|lt=Setouchi, Okayama|1=Setouchi, Okayama}} Ushimado Town, Kashino |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} |type={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |map_size=Length: 55m
Height: 5m (both rear and front sections) |Burial Facility=Single-sided horizontal stone chamber |burial goods=Rock crystal Magatama, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} |built=Late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|1=Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11472269}}, designated in 1956 |image= }} '''Futatsukayama Kofun''' (二塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|en|Setouchi, Okayama|qid=Q691691|lt=Setouchi, Okayama|1=Setouchi, Okayama}}, Ushimado Town, Kashino, Okayama Prefecture. It is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Keyhole-shaped tumuli in Ushimado Bay !location within bay!!name!!Length!!built!!Historic Site Designation |- |Northeast||{{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}||85m||Mid to late 4th century||Setouchi City Historic Site |- |South||{{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}} (No.1)||81m||Early to mid-5th century||None |- |Northwest||{{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kaboyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaboyama Kofun|qq=}}||84m||Late 5th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |- |North||{{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikayama Kofun|qq=}}||60m||Early 6th century||Demolished |- |Southwest||'''Futatsukayama Kofun'''||55m||Late 6th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |}
Futatsukayama Kofun is situated on a hill overlooking the western end of Ushimado Bay, guarding the bay’s entrance. In Ushimado Bay, five keyhole-shaped tumuli, including Futatsukayama, {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kaboyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaboyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikayama Kofun|qq=}} (now destroyed), {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}, were constructed to encircle the bay. Futatsukayama Kofun is the westernmost of these and has not undergone archaeological excavation to date. The kofun has a keyhole shape, with its front section oriented southeast toward the sea. The mound lacks terraced construction. Cylindrical and shield-shaped {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} were found on the mound’s surface, but no paving stones were detected. The burial facility consists of a single-sided horizontal stone chamber in the rear circular section. The chamber measures over 12 meters in total length, with the main chamber measuring 7 meters in length, 2 meters in width, and 1.7 meters in height. Artifacts discovered include rock crystal Magatama, haniwa, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun dates back to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, estimated around the late 6th century. Among the five keyhole-shaped tumuli in Ushimado, it is the most recently constructed, reflecting the political significance of Ushimado Bay as a port during this period. The kofun site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1956. == Mound Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows: * Total length: approximately 55 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approximately 30–35 meters ** Height: approximately 5 meters * Narrow section width: approximately 23 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: approximately 25 meters ** Width: approximately 30 meters ** Height: approximately 5 meters File:Futatsukayama Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Stone materials at the rear circular section mound File:Futatsukayama Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Futatsukayama Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1956[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=362 Futatsukayama Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Setouchi City official site).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * Explanatory panel at the site (Setouchi City Board of Education) * Publications by local authorities ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=Futatsukayama Kofun|title=Ushimado Town History Materials II: Archaeology, Ancient, Medieval, and Early Modern|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=|pages=174–184}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Futatsukayama Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639|title=Ushimado Town Ancient Kofuns Chart|publisher=Ushimado Town Board of Education|page=91}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Futatsukayama Kofun|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490344}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Futatsukayama Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=362 Futatsukayama Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture "Cultural Properties of Okayama") * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/kenjyuyo/1418023774171.html Futatsukayama Kofun] (Setouchi City official site) {{Kofun navbar}} [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] [[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]] [[Template:infobox ancient site]] [[File:Futatsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The hill where the kofun is located (back right)}}{{small|Seen from Ushimado Olive Garden. Left above: {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; center right: {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kaboyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaboyama Kofun|qq=}} and Futatsukayama Kofun; central hill: former location of {{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikayama Kofun|qq=}}.}}]] [[ja:二塚山古墳_(瀬戸内市)]] [[ja:二塚山古墳]] {{Commons category|Futatsukayama Kofun}} [[ja:二塚山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] {{translated page|ja|二塚山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=95137563|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q55523155}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
